fragility_points: Single-minute settlement-window volatility on one exchange.
Weekend or macro-driven drawdown that compresses the current price cushion quickly.
key_assumptions: The current ~75k Binance level persists well enough that only a meaningful downside move threatens settlement above 70000.
Binance provides a clean and representative 12:00 ET 1-minute close on April 20.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket defines the contract by the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close on April 20.
Binance direct data currently shows BTC/USDT near 75k, well above the 70k threshold.
That cushion makes Yes highly likely unless a sizable downside move or Binance-specific settlement anomaly occurs before resolution.
main_thesis: BTC/USDT is already far enough above 70000 on Binance that this is a high-probability Yes unless a meaningful downside shock hits before the April 20 noon ET settlement minute.
source_quality_view: High-quality direct evidence from Binance carries the thesis; contextual media evidence is secondary and mainly useful for catalyst mapping.
strongest_disconfirmers: Crypto can move more than 7% in four days if macro risk or ETF-flow sentiment turns sharply.
A single-minute Binance settlement print is more path-sensitive than a daily-close contract.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance ticker during the run showed BTCUSDT at 75042.98, about 5043 points above strike.
Recent sampled Binance daily closes were also comfortably above 70000.
The contract requires only one specific 1-minute close above 70000, not a sustained multi-day hold.
timing_relevance: The key issue is persistence of the current >70k regime into Monday noon ET, not the need for a new bullish catalyst.
unresolved_ambiguities: Minor operational ambiguity remains around exact Binance UI/API candle presentation, though not enough to change the directional view.
what_would_change_view: A sharp move toward or below 70k before settlement, or signs of Binance-specific settlement-minute instability, would reduce confidence materially.
